title: bromus muralis var. madritensis
intro: Bromus muralis var. madritensis, also known as Spanish Brome, is a perennial grass native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Spanish Brome are thought to include reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Spanish Brome can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
